poems.title: That Glorious Arrow Must Still Be in Flight / Cast toward the Distant West
lines: The blood-red setting sun is about to die
The dark-gold curtain of night about to fall
Zhang Qian drives his herded flock homeward
A desolate pastoral song blends with the mournful reed-pipe
Piercing the haze of an autumn twilight
From a distant felt tent a thread of smoke rises
Like a dark-gold peony from his native Hanzhong
Slowly blooming across the sky of the Western Regions
He turns to gaze at that dark-gold cloud far to the west
The flock surges like waves beneath his horse
Yet that glorious arrow must still be in flight
Cast toward the distant road ahead
Of the Great Yuezhi, longed for day and night

annotations.note: Zhang Qian — Han Dynasty diplomat and explorer (c. 164-114 BC) who pioneered the Silk Road route to Central Asia on behalf of Emperor Wu.
古代北方游牧民族的簧管乐器，音色苍凉悲壮，常用于边塞诗歌意象。
Great Yuezhi — a nomadic people originally from the Gansu/Tarim region, later migrated to Central Asia (modern Afghanistan/Pakistan).
Han Staff (jié) — the imperial envoy's staff of authority, a bamboo rod topped with yak-tail tassels symbolizing the emperor's mandate.

poems.title: The Clasped Hands Slowly Loosened / The Tear-Filled Eyes Abruptly Turned Away
lines: The boundless steppe has fallen asleep
The child in the felt tent must have fallen asleep too
At his weeping wife of the Western Regions
Her curling, tawny hair drifts in the breeze
Her eyes, blue as Lake Qinghai, gleam with tears
Her tears, ripe as grapes, fall
The black steed stamps the grass without ceasing
And a tall stack of dried naan still hang from the saddle
Zhang Qian gazes in silence at
The wife of the Western Regions with whom he has lived for years
The tear-filled eyes abruptly turn away

annotations.note: 祁连山，位于甘肃、青海交界，匈奴语意为「天山」。是河西走廊的南部屏障，古代中原通往西域的必经之地。

poems.title: O Great Yuezhi / The Holy Land He Has Dreamed of for Eleven Springs
lines: How long he has been riding, he does not know
Whether he is still riding, he is not sure
In the darkness, not even the dim horizon is visible
All seems frozen in the profound dark
Still he presses on with all his strength
The distance between him and Tangyi Fu slowly narrows
But where is the mysterious Great Yuezhi?
Where is the distant Great Yuezhi?
Just follow loyal, capable Tangyi Fu
Just ride into the pitch-black west
One day they shall reach their destination
The holy land he has dreamed of for eleven springs
Through more than four thousand unbearable days in foreign lands
Through more than four thousand unbearable nights in foreign lands
From the galloping saddle he turns once more
To gaze at a silhouette already lost from sight

annotations.note: Tangyi Fu — Zhang Qian's loyal Xiongnu companion and guide throughout his journey to the Western Regions.

poems.title: Bathed in the Cool Radiance / That Daylight Had Yearned for Like Shade
lines: When did it sheathe its scorching golden arrows?
A crescent moon above the distant western Tianshan
Waking from a burning, dreadful dream
He bathes in the cool, liquid light
That daylight had yearned for like shade
He clenches his fist ever tighter
Trickle slowly through his fingers to the ground
He can hear the soft rustling sound
As though the mountain stream of home flows again before him

poems.title: The Pioneer of the Silk Road Smiled from Horseback / Gazing Westward
lines: No longer narrow as the Hexi Corridor
As though on a ship of the Gobi
As though listening to the distant melody of camel bells
O, the new continent of Central Asia
Appeared before the explorer bearing the Han envoy's staff
The pioneer of the Silk Road smiled from horseback
From his cracked lips, crimson drops of blood trickled down

annotations.note: 位于甘肃省西北部、祁连山与北山之间的狭长通道，是中原通往西域的咽喉要道，丝绸之路的关键路段。
Silk Road — the ancient network of trade routes connecting China to the Mediterranean, named for the Chinese silk traded westward.

poems.title: He Kept His Eyes Fixed / On the Distant Icy Peaks of Qilian
lines: He can no longer bear to see
On the distant icy peaks of Qilian
Floating on the surging, ash-brown clouds
Now and then flashing a blue, spectral light
Huo Qubing licked his cracked lips

annotations.note: Huo Qubing — brilliant young general (140-117 BC) who defeated the Xiongnu at Qilian. Died at age 24.

poems.title: Is That the Long-Dreamed Crescent Moon Lake? / The Crescent Lake That Once Graced His Dreams?
lines: Iron-grey thickets, not yet budding with elm coins,
The night sky, too, is iron-grey
Those startled pigeons are iron-grey too
The beating of their wings fades in an instant
Glimmering with a pale, spectral light
Is that the Crescent Moon Lake he has long dreamed of?
The crescent lake that once graced his dreams?

annotations.note: Dunhuang — an ancient oasis city at the western end of the Hexi Corridor, famous for its Mogao Caves and Buddhist art.
即月牙泉，位于敦煌鸣沙山下，因形似新月而得名，自古被誉为「沙漠第一泉」。

poems.title: The Carrier Pigeon Circled Hurriedly for a Moment / Then Flew Eastward
lines: When he hastily tore open his black tunic
A carrier pigeon rose from his breast
Had already stained the white plumage crimson
Skimming past the steeds that galloped by the soldier
Past the sand-grains lifted like mist
Past the overcast sky, dull as sand
Lying face-up on the embattled earth
Iron cavalry thundering across his body
Like storm clouds sweeping before his eyes
Yet his eyes still gaze at the vast heavens
The carrier pigeon circled hurriedly for a moment
Across the reflection in his pupils — of flashing blades and gleaming swords —

poems.title: O, Son of the Tianshan / Torrent of Snow-Melt
lines: Rises like a red cloud from the saddle
Even the retreating enemy riders turn to look —
As though a red lotus had bloomed
At the war drum in your breast
Four hundred leagues of wilderness spring into green
In the wake of galloping hooves

poems.title: His Phantom-Like Black Steed / Stands Frozen as a Statue Beneath Qilian
lines: Stands frozen as a statue beneath Qilian
He still gazes at the icy peak before him
As though that peak were his eternal monument
He still gazes at the grasslands before him
As though those grasslands were his eternal bed
The wind whispers against his crimson cloak
Like the rustling of silk banners
Like the murmur of his mother's lullaby
He was only twenty-four when he
Let the reins slip from his fingers
When he closed his eyes for the last time
But the war drum in his breast still thunders
Through the vast deserts and grasslands of the Western Regions

poems.title: Standing in the Wine-Sweet, Dark-Gold Sunset / Standing in the Future Bazaars of the Silk Road
lines: The setting sun paused a moment before slipping away
Still gilding the young general's bronze cheeks
The wind-worn, time-struck features of his face
Still bore the flush of that last battle
His war horse bent down to drink from the spring
Like the silk road unfurling toward the west
Standing in the wine-sweet, dark-gold sunset
He could smell the fragrance drifting from the future
The mingled scent of grapes and pomegranates
Standing in the future bazaars of the Silk Road
Standing in streets paved with moonlight and camel bells
Let the wine of Jiuquan pour like gold
Into the spring that the emperor had named for him
For his soldiers to share, one sip at a time

annotations.note: Jiuquan ('Wine Spring') — city in Gansu named after a legend that Huo Qubing poured wine into a spring to share with his troops.

poems.title: Beneath the Galloping Hooves / Waves of Wild Poppies Unfurl
lines: She rides from the deep grassland
From blooming wild poppies, jade-green and crimson
Like two young swallows in flight
Crimson and boundless as the sunset
With the wind in her face
How far away the Central Plains must be
Yet flowers of the Central Plains bloom here too
There are no walls on this steppe
And the Ili River, winding southward

annotations.note: 位于新疆伊犁州新源县，天山山脉中段的高山草甸，水草丰茂，是古代乌孙国的核心牧地。
即刘细君，西汉江都王刘建之女。元封六年（前105年），汉武帝将她作为和亲公主远嫁乌孙国昆莫（国王），史称「细君公主」。
Wusun — ancient nomadic kingdom in the Ili River valley (modern Kazakhstan/Xinjiang) allied with the Han Dynasty.

poems.title: The July Night Is / So Hushed and Still
lines: Even the Ili River has quieted its murmur
Their crimson petals glistening with dew
Like tears she has not yet shed
She stands beneath the Tianshan moon
Already smells of home — of earth
She cannot tell whether it is the steppe or her heart that sighs

annotations.note: Ili River — major river flowing from the Tianshan into Lake Balkhash, historically central to Wusun and Kazakh cultures.

poems.title: A Dark-Brown Wild Poppy Has Withered / Yet Its Petals Still Release a Heady Fragrance
lines: A dark-brown wild poppy has withered
Yet its petals still release a heady fragrance
Time has turned the crimson to brown
She pressed the petals between the pages
Of a letter she would send to Chang'an
But there was no courier to the east
Stayed in the valley of the Ili
Even now, two thousand years later

poems.title: Before Her, the Ili River / Has No Trace of That Crimson Leaf That Carried Her Childhood Heart
lines: On the day she left Chang'an
A child pressed a red leaf into her hand
She placed it on the water of the Wei River
Across deserts she had never imagined
The leaf in her memory kept drifting
She never saw that leaf again —
That red leaf carrying her childhood heart
Has no trace of that crimson leaf
Only the fallen petals of wild poppies
His felt tent smells of mutton and fermented mare's milk
Through the tent flap she can see
Her childhood is buried in Chang'an
Her girlhood lost along the road
Here at the edge of the known world
She is a princess with no kingdom to return to
Before her, the Ili River flows on
They are not her crimson leaf

poems.title: The Mournful Phrases of Her 'Song of Longing for Home' / Still Echo
lines: My family married me to the edge of the sky —
Her song drifts from two thousand years ago
Over the steppe, over the river
My dwelling is a felt tent, its walls of wool
My food is flesh, my drink is milk —
The words she sang have seeped into the earth
Into the roots of every wild poppy
I long to be a yellow swan
But the yellow swan does not fly eastward
It circles above the Ili River
Above the snow peaks of the Tianshan
The first poem of the Silk Road
But in tears and wild poppy petals
Still bloom on the banks of the Ili
The mournful phrases of her 'Song of Longing for Home'
When the wild poppies bloom once more

annotations.note: 细君公主所作的《悲愁歌》：「吾家嫁我兮天一方，远托异国兮乌孙王。穹庐为室兮旃为墙，以肉为食兮酪为浆。居常土思兮心内伤，愿为黄鹄兮归故乡。」是中国文学史上最早的和亲诗歌。
即天鹅，细君公主《悲愁歌》中以「愿为黄鹄兮归故乡」表达思乡之情，此后黄鹄成为思乡的经典意象。
哈萨克语，意为「红花」，指新疆伊犁河谷常见的野生郁金香，春季盛开如火。

poems.title: The Commander on Horseback / A Bitter Smile Crosses His Lips
lines: Sixty thousand troops set out toward the west
Bound for a kingdom most had never heard of
Bound for horses that sweated blood
Brother of the emperor's beloved consort
A man more accustomed to the court than the saddle
Now master of an army stretched thin as silk
Before it reached the walls of Dayuan
Starved, thirsty, beaten by the desert
With barely a tenth of his men
A bitter smile crosses his lips
Return. Bring back the horses. Or do not return at all.
He gazes at the Pamir snowcaps
At the sky above the Tarim
At the fallen soldiers whose bones

annotations.note: Li Guangli — Han general who led the disastrous Dayuan expeditions (104-101 BC) to obtain the famed 'blood-sweating' horses.
Blood-Sweating Horses — the famed Akhal-Teke horses of Fergana whose skin appeared to 'sweat blood' during exertion.
Dayuan (Fergana) — an ancient kingdom in Central Asia's Fergana Valley, famed for its 'blood-sweating' Akhal-Teke horses.
帕米尔高原，位于中亚东南部，古称「葱岭」，是古代丝绸之路翻越的最高屏障，连接中国与中亚。

poems.title: Then Suddenly Froze Amid the Flood of Men / Frozen into the Shape of a Blood-Sweating Horse, Neighing Skyward
lines: The gates of Dayuan opened at last
Not to diplomacy, but to the sword
Inside the walls, the fabled horses
Stamped and snorted in their stalls
Their flanks glistened with crimson moisture
As though they wept blood in the heat
These were the horses the emperor dreamed of?
Three thousand horses were led out
Their hooves striking the stone road
Like a drumroll from the heart of the earth
One horse reared suddenly amid the flood of men
Frozen into the shape of a blood-sweating horse
As though even it knew the price that had been paid

poems.title: Toward the Distant West / Toward the Unforgettable West
lines: Three thousand horses in a river of dust
Through the iron-cold nights of the Pamir
The horses of Dayuan, unaccustomed to this land
Stumbled on the gravel of the Taklamakan
Some fell and did not rise
Their blood seeping into the sand
— Red as the sweat they once shed when they ran
The soldiers sang no songs on this road
They had sung them all on the way out
And the distant creaking of the supply carts
Li Guangli counted the horses each dawn
Each dawn the number was smaller
As though the desert were drinking them
He thought of the Amu River
Where the water was sweet and the grass was green
Where the Yuezhi people once grazed their flocks
Before Zhang Qian came, before anyone came
He had traveled with the force of empire behind him
With an empire's worth of grief ahead
How many horses would survive to reach Chang'an?
Their crimson sweat drying in the desert wind
Like the last ink on a silk letter

annotations.note: 中国最大的沙漠，位于新疆塔里木盆地中心，维吾尔语意为「进去出不来的地方」，面积约33万平方公里。
帕米尔高原上的重要山口，位于中国与原苏联（今吉尔吉斯斯坦）边界，是古代丝绸之路的西出通道。
中亚最长河流，古称「妫水」，流经阿富汗、塔吉克斯坦、乌兹别克斯坦和土库曼斯坦。大月氏西迁后定居于阿姆河流域。

poems.title: Already He Can See / The Solemn, Gentle Towers of Chang'an
lines: The solemn, gentle towers of Chang'an
The emperor waits at the Xuanwu Gate
His ministers in their silken robes
The incense burners sending blue spirals skyward
Of the three thousand blood-sweating horses
Only a thousand have survived the road
But now more bone than muscle
The emperor will compose his 'Song of the Heavenly Horses' —
'Their spirit is extraordinary, their power divine' —
And have it performed with bells and jade chimes
But the sound of horses falling in the sand
But the silence after the last soldier's cry
Now stand in the imperial stables
Like the tears of every man who did not return

annotations.note: 西汉第七位皇帝刘彻（前156—前87），在位五十四年。雄才大略，开拓西域，派张骞出使，使丝绸之路正式贯通。
汉武帝获得汗血马后亲作的乐府诗，赞美天马「志俶傥，精权奇，籋浮云，晻上驰」，抒发帝王的宏图壮志。

poems.title: Prostrate beside Lop Nur / Prostrate beneath the Bonfire of the High Altar
lines: Prostrate beneath the bonfire of the high altar
The priest of Loulan chants in an unknown tongue
His shadow stretching into the salt flats
The lake that once mirrored the Kunlun peaks
Cracking like the bones of fish
Dead a thousand years but still upright
As though waiting for the water to return
But by the slow turning of a river's course

annotations.note: Lop Nur — once a large salt lake in Xinjiang, now a dry lake bed. Site of the Loulan ruins and nuclear testing.
又称「胡桐」，耐旱耐盐碱的落叶乔木，大量分布于塔克拉玛干沙漠周缘。有「生而千年不死，死而千年不倒，倒而千年不朽」之说。
又称红柳，西北荒漠中常见的灌木或小乔木，耐盐碱、耐干旱，是防风固沙的重要植物。
Loulan — ancient Silk Road kingdom near Lop Nur, vanished around 4th century AD due to desertification.

poems.title: This Seed Gathered from the Forests of Lop Nur / Still Damp with Night Dew
lines: This seed gathered from the forests of Lop Nur
He carried it a thousand leagues to the east
Wrapped in a scrap of Loulan silk
Would it remember the salt-crusted shore?
Stood at the crossroads of civilizations
Where silk went west and jade came east
Where languages tangled in the bazaar
He pressed the seed to his cheek
It was cool, like the nights by the lake
Cool, like the skin of his daughter
Among the ruins of a kingdom

poems.title: O, Let the Immortal Sound of Lop Nur's Waves / Echo Forever in the Dreams of the Homeland
lines: O, let the immortal sound of Lop Nur's waves
Echo forever in the dreams of the homeland
Though the waves have long since stilled
Though the lake bed crackles underfoot
Like a song the sand refuses to forget
Like a road the wind cannot erase
Let the poplar's ghost stand witness
Let the tamarisk hold the dune in place
Until the river, in some far century,

poems.title: Crescent-Shaped Lop Nur / Already Set at the Far Edge of a Distant Sky
lines: Where the jade was weighed and the silk was folded
Where the storyteller told of mountains to the west
Only the dry rattle of dead branches
Already set at the far edge of a distant sky
But a memory of a lake
And brushed the sand from Loulan's face
After four thousand years beneath the salt
In a glass case in Ürümqi
Over the place she was born
But the memory of water and light

poems.title: The Green Flame of the Mountain Pasture / Already Burns in the Herdsman's Heart
lines: Nineteen years at the shore of the North Sea
Nineteen years with a staff of withered bamboo
Its yak-tail pennant long since frayed to nothing
Su Wu tends his flock of rams
On the frozen shore of Lake Baikal
Where no message arrives from the south
When the ice breaks and the green flame
Of new grass sweeps across the steppe
He feels it burn in his heart too
The green flame of the mountain pasture
Already burns in the herdsman's heart
Not the heart of a herdsman — no —
The heart of a Han envoy
His robes have rotted to rags
His hair has silvered like the birch bark
Though bare of pennant and authority
The lake has frozen and thawed nineteen times

annotations.note: Lake Baikal — the world's deepest and oldest freshwater lake in Siberia, where Su Wu was exiled.
Su Wu — Han Dynasty envoy (c. 140-60 BC) who was held captive by the Xiongnu for 19 years at Lake Baikal, refusing to surrender.

poems.title: Kissing This Handful of Earth for a Long Time / His Longing Eyes Gently Closed
lines: When they told him he could go home
He did not believe them at first
Had taught him not to trust words
But then the southern envoy showed him
Like the spring ice shattering on the lake
He knelt on the frozen ground
And scooped up a handful of Baikal earth
Black and cold and smelling of pine
Kissing this handful of earth for a long time
The sound of your wind on winter nights
He placed the earth in his sleeve
The flock of rams watched him go
What it means to be faithful

poems.title: Standing on the Shore of Crystalline Baikal / Like a Stern Iceberg
lines: Li Ling came to see him at the farewell feast
Li Ling — grandson of the Flying General
Li Ling — who had also fought and lost
Two old men who had been young warriors
Two men who had served the same emperor
One who bent, one who did not
Li Ling said: Stay with me.
The Xiongnu princess treats me well.
I have sons now. A warm tent.
Standing on the shore of crystalline Baikal
And drank to the south wind
But for his own inability to follow
They parted at the lake's edge
One riding south with a bare staff
One remaining with a borrowed name

annotations.note: Li Ling — grandson of the famous general Li Guang, who surrendered to the Xiongnu after a heroic last stand.

poems.title: That Wild Goose Flew from His Hand / From His Blood-Stained Hand
lines: A wild goose carried his message home
That the emperor's hunters shot it from the sky
And found a silk letter tied to its leg
Su Wu is alive, the letter said
He is at the North Sea
That wild goose flew from his hand
A man it had given up for dead
When Su Wu walked through the city gates at last
The common people lined the streets and wept
Not because he was a hero

annotations.note: 苏武在北海牧羊时，传说汉昭帝使者对匈奴称：天子射猎时射得鸿雁，足上系有苏武帛书。匈奴遂释放苏武。此即「鸿雁传书」典故之由来。

poems.title: The Flock of Doves Circling Above Yangguan / Still Circles in Her Eyes
lines: The flock of doves circling above Yangguan
The Silk Princess hides her secret
In the golden crown upon her head
She carries the silkworm eggs westward
Past the Jade Gate, past the sentries
Past the desert where only wind lives
The mulberry trees will soon be planted
From the looms of the Western Regions
Who taught the West to weave?
A princess who loved a foreign king
And smuggled the seeds of civilization
At Yangguan, the doves still circle
For a girl who passed through long ago

annotations.note: 古代西域地名，今新疆莎车县一带。叶尔羌河是塔里木河的主要支流之一。
传说中将养蚕和丝织技术带到西域的中原公主。据和田出土的木版画记载，一位嫁往于阗（今和田）的公主将蚕种藏于凤冠中偷渡出关，从此丝织业在西域传播开来。
位于甘肃敦煌西南，汉代设置的边关要塞，是丝绸之路南道的必经之地。王维诗「西出阳关无故人」使其闻名遐迩。

poems.title: That Ash-Brown Horizon / Moving Slowly Forward
lines: Beyond what the eye can hold
Beyond what the heart can bear
At the towers receding into dust
At the land she will never see again

poems.title: The Crescent Moon Above the Great Mosque / May Also Glow with a Pale, Mysterious Radiance
lines: The crescent moon above the great mosque
May also glow with a pale, mysterious radiance
Where silk and jade have made
In every tongue that travels the road

poems.title: That Song Dissolves into the Waves of the Yarkand River / And the Symphony of Rustling Mulberry Groves
lines: She taught them to unwind the cocoon
Thread by thread, patient as prayer
The women of Khotan learned quickly
Their dark fingers nimble on the loom
From the mulberry groves that she had planted
The silkworms spun their golden thread
That song dissolves into the waves
And the symphony of rustling mulberry groves
The looms of Khotan wove on

poems.title: The Amber Glow of Silk / Shining in King Axihan's Amber-Colored Dream
lines: Gazing east toward the shimmering road
He had asked for a bride from the Han court
And they had sent him a princess
With a secret sewn into her crown
And the tiny eggs were found
For she had brought not just herself
The mulberry groves spread across Khotan
And the Silk Road earned its name
At the foot of the Kunlun Mountains
In the amber light of sunset
In the Yarkand River's last reflection
Before the desert drinks it dry

annotations.note: 传说中的于阗国王，迎娶了从中原远嫁而来、偷带蚕种的丝绸公主。

poems.title: Drowning Out Even the Thunderous Hoofbeats / Like a Storm
lines: Thirty-six men rode into the Western Regions
Led by Ban Chao, a scholar turned soldier
A man who had traded the brush for the sword
Who said: 'He who does not enter the tiger's den / Cannot catch the tiger's cub'
At Shanshan, the first test came
The Xiongnu envoys had arrived before him
Outnumbering his men ten to one
That night he lit fires around the Xiongnu camp
And his men roared like an army of thousands
Drowning out even the thunderous hoofbeats
By dawn, the Xiongnu envoys were dead
Thirty years he stayed in the western lands
At the edge of the world

annotations.note: Ban Chao — Han Dynasty general (32-102 AD) who, with only 36 men, re-established Chinese authority across the Western Regions.
Shanshan — a Silk Road kingdom near Lop Nur; Ban Chao's first victory in the Western Regions.

poems.title: As Though a Hundred Thousand Armored Men / A Mournful, Desolate Melody Flows
lines: In his old age, Ban Chao asked to go home
His letter to the emperor read:
'I dare not hope to reach Jiuquan alive
I only wish my bones may cross the Jade Gate'
As though a hundred thousand armored men
Through the pass, through the corridor
Through the thirty years of exile
He died within a month of his return
As though his body had been waiting
Only for the sight of home

poems.title: Already Locked in Close Combat / Killing in Silence
lines: After Ban Chao left, the beacons dimmed
Like a string of jade beads
The silence of an empire retreating

poems.title: The Morning Star Is Fading / The Beacon-Fires Have Died
lines: Along the road from Dunhuang to Kashgar
Or been buried in the sand
Worn into the earth by centuries
Of silk and jade and spice
Of armies and monks and merchants
Over the passes of the Pamir
Over the plains of Central Asia

poems.title: Praying in Silence toward the Distant West / That Holy Land
lines: Gan Ying stood at the edge of the known world
Where the land ended and the sea began
Praying in silence toward the distant west
That holy land — Rome — Da Qin
The Parthian merchants shook their heads:
'The sea is vast. The voyage takes months.'
He gazed at the dark-blue water
The waves that separated him from Rome
From the western edge of the Silk Road
From the empire that wore the silk
The farthest envoy China had sent

annotations.note: Gan Ying — Han envoy sent by Ban Chao to reach Da Qin (Rome) in 97 AD. Reached the Persian Gulf but was turned back.

poems.title: Through the Branches Where the Golden Partridges Perched / Heavy Red Rain Falls with a Rustling Sound
lines: Through the branches where the golden partridges perched
Heavy red rain falls with a rustling sound
Gan Ying walked these foreign gardens
Smelling scents he had no names for
Tasting fruit that grew in no Chinese earth
The Parthian king received him with curiosity
'You come from the land of silk?'
Gan Ying calculated in his mind:
From Luoyang to here — forty thousand li
'From where the sun rises to where it begins to set.'
Fragrant as a road without end

poems.title: The Wind from the Zagros Mountains, Damp / Blowing toward Him with a Briny Tang
lines: The wind from the Zagros Mountains, damp
Blowing toward him with a briny tang
Carried whispers of the sea beyond
Could almost see its marble columns
But the Parthians would not let him cross
They held the middle of the road
The brokers of silk and glass

annotations.note: 伊朗高原西部的大山脉，从波斯湾北岸延伸至土耳其东部，是古代丝绸之路通往两河流域的天然屏障。
位于阿拉伯半岛与伊朗高原之间的海湾。甘英到达波斯湾后被告知渡海需数月，遂未能继续前往罗马。

poems.title: Bitter Seawater Mingled with Bitter Tears / Cast Again into the Dark-Blue Persian Gulf
lines: He stood at the water's edge a long time
The waves breaking over his worn leather boots
Bitter seawater mingled with bitter tears
Cast again into the dark-blue Persian Gulf
Through the sands of the Taklamakan
Whose names even scholars had forgotten
And here, at the threshold of Rome
'The sea will take two years,' they said
Knowing that if East met West directly
His shadow falling on the Persian Gulf
The longest shadow China ever cast westward
Of glass that could be blown like breath
Of an empire that matched his own
Two empires, ignorant of each other

poems.title: His Three Thousand Dunhuang Soldiers / Also Fell Prostrate on the Riverbank
lines: Suo Le led three thousand men from Dunhuang
To the banks of the Tarim River
Dark water rushing from the melting snows
Of mountains they could not see
Also fell prostrate on the riverbank
Pressing their faces to the water
Drinking as though they would never drink again
Then they rose and began to farm
These soldiers who had carried swords
The first military farms of the Western Regions
Guarded by men who missed their homes
Into the cities of the frontier

annotations.note: 中国最长的内陆河，全长约2137公里，流经新疆塔里木盆地北缘。古代是西域屯田戍边的命脉之河。
东汉将领，率敦煌兵三千人进驻西域，强渡塔里木河，在其南岸开创了中原王朝在西域腹地屯田的先河。
军队开垦荒地、从事农业生产的制度。始于西汉在西域的「屯田」政策，是巩固边疆的重要举措，也是新疆生产建设兵团的历史渊源。

poems.title: Eyes Gently Closed / A Green Cloud Drifts from the Distance
lines: A green cloud drifts from the distance
In his dream, the Tarim shore
Fields of wheat stretch to the horizon
He sees a future he cannot live to see
A future of orchards and cotton
Of children who will never know
The sound of sword on shield
Who wants peace more than glory

poems.title: Merging with the Dark Waves of the Tarim / Surging Ceaselessly under the Pale Moonlight
lines: In a language older than any empire
Merging with the dark waves of the Tarim
Surging ceaselessly under the pale moonlight
'It reminds me of the willows by the Wei River.'

poems.title: Anaerguli on Horseback / Gazing into the Distance
lines: The Uyghur girl rides along the canal
She does not know their names
But drinks the water they channeled
Han and Uyghur, soldier and farmer
All drinking from the same river
All gazing at the same moon

poems.title: Still Gazing Down / Still Gazing Down
lines: From the cliffs above the Tarim
He has seen the armies come and go
At the river that never stops
At the desert that never sleeps
At the road that never ends
The waves of the Tarim River

poems.title: Is This the Night at the Frontier? / So Hushed and Still
lines: Is this the night at the frontier?
Only the distant call of a night bird
And the rustle of sand grains shifting
Xuanzang pressed his body against the cold earth
A fugitive from his own empire
A monk who dared defy the emperor's ban
His crime: to seek the truth
In a land beyond the horizon

annotations.note: 此处指唐代高僧玄奘（602—664），此组诗以「偷渡的托钵僧」为题，因玄奘当年私自出关西行求法，身份实为偷渡者。

poems.title: Tear-Drops Gleam in the Starlight / So Full of Feeling
lines: As the monk looks back one last time
At the dark outline of the Great Wall
He will not see it again
Through the furnace of the desert
Through kingdoms of ice and stone
But of a man who loves his home
So deeply that he must leave it

poems.title: The Graceful Flying Apsara / Scatters Rose Petals Damp with Dew
lines: At the Mogao Caves he prayed
Scatters rose petals damp with dew
Their scarves floating in painted wind
Their smiles eternal on the sandstone walls
The road is long but the truth is worth it.
He lit a candle in the darkness
And touched his forehead to the cold stone floor
One thousand three hundred thirty-five volumes
On the back of a single monk

poems.title: Yi Yi Yi / This Is a Cloud Damp with Mist
lines: Where mirages danced like cruel promises
This is a cloud damp with mist
Of the world's most remorseless desert
Toward the sound of temple bells

poems.title: He Prays by Moonlight with Eyes Closed / Yet Before Him Floats a Green River
lines: He prays by moonlight with eyes closed
Yet before him floats a green river
The king begged him to stay
But the river in his vision flowed on
Past the limits of the map
Into the unmapped country of the soul

poems.title: The Waves of Issyk-Kul / Still Surging and Splashing in His Mind
lines: At Issyk-Kul the water was warm
Still surging and splashing in his mind
Yabgu Khan of the Western Turks
Received him with feasts and honor
And a letter of safe passage
Xuanzang, the mendicant who crossed the border in secret
Now traveled with an escort fit for an emperor
The greatest university the world had known
He studied there for five years
The journey home took another seven years
Through the very desert that had nearly killed him
But now he carried the scriptures
One thousand three hundred thirty-five volumes
On the back of a single monk
The mendicant who crossed the border in secret

annotations.note: 古代安西四镇之一，位于今吉尔吉斯斯坦托克马克附近，唐代西域重镇。传为诗人李白出生地。
Issyk-Kul — an endorheic lake in Kyrgyzstan, the second largest alpine lake in the world.
西突厥可汗，玄奘西行至中亚时曾受其热情接待。叶护可汗赐予护卫和通关文书，助玄奘继续西行。
Xuanzang — Tang Dynasty Buddhist monk (602-664) whose 17-year pilgrimage to India inspired Journey to the West.

poems.title: O, No Longer a Solitary Pilgrim / A Warm Current Dispels the Bitter Cold
lines: Cen Can arrived at the frontier in autumn
When the first snow dusted the Tianshan
O, no longer a solitary pilgrim
A warm current dispels the bitter cold
For here was a poet among soldiers
A man of brushes amid men of swords
Who saw beauty in the terrible:
'The north wind sweeps the earth, snapping white grasses,
In the eighth month, snow falls on the Tartar frontier —
Like a spring gale scattering blossoms of pear,'
He saw them not as hardship
This was the poet of the frontier
Across the pages of Tang Dynasty literature
Cen Can — whose soul still gallops

annotations.note: Cen Can — Tang Dynasty frontier poet (c. 715-770), famous for his vivid descriptions of Silk Road landscapes and border warfare.

poems.title: With the Hand That Had Swung the Saber / He Hastily Caressed the Poem-Pouch at His Waist
lines: With the hand that had swung the saber
He hastily caressed the poem-pouch at his waist
That leather satchel where he kept his drafts
That Zhang Qian had navigated by
That Su Wu had wept beneath
He dipped his brush in battlefield ink
And wrote of snow and fire
Of a land so beautiful and so brutal
That only poetry could contain it
The cry of a northern flute

poems.title: The Orange Campfire Still Gleams / A New Star Added to the Night Sky of Central Asia
lines: A new star added to the night sky of Central Asia
Cen Can recites his verses to the soldiers
Who listen with tears in their eyes
That their suffering could be beautiful
That their exile could become legend
That a poet's brush could dignify
The horses stamp in the cold
And somewhere in the dark beyond
To the human voices at their feet

poems.title: So Still You Can Hear / The Poet's Teardrops Falling
lines: When Cen Can left the frontier
He looked back at the Tianshan
He had arrived fearing the wilderness
He had arrived a minor official
The cold, the distance, the loneliness
The echo of his brush on paper
Through the passes of the Tianshan

poems.title: Like the Crimson Paper-Cut His Wife Made / On Their Wedding Night
lines: Of his wife's face in lamplight
Like the crimson paper-cut his wife made
And placed it in his poem-pouch
Against the cold of the frontier
Home carried in a leather satchel

poems.title: He Seems to See His Cavalry Charging / Along the Banks of the Talas River
lines: Where the Tang Empire met the Abbasid Caliphate
Where East met West in blood and fire
He seems to see his cavalry charging
Along the banks of the Talas River
The last great push of Chinese power westward
But from the prisoners of that battle
To Baghdad, to Cairo, to Europe
And the world was changed forever

poems.title: Listening to the Unforgettable Summer of Talas / A Requiem So Mournful Yet Tender
lines: By the Talas River in summer
Listening to the unforgettable summer of Talas
A requiem so mournful yet tender
For the soldiers who fell here
Whose bones became the river's bed
Who taught his captors the art
Of pressing mulberry bark into sheets
Than the sword that captured him
Listen — the Talas River still whispers
The sound of water over paper
The sound of a world transforming

poems.title: A Blue-Brown Speck of Light / Sweeping Across the Chaotic Earth and Chaotic Sky
lines: Sweeping across the chaotic earth and chaotic sky
Through the carnage and the smoke

poems.title: O, the Crescent Moon and Morning Star / The Crescent Moon and Morning Star of Islam
lines: After Talas, the crescent moon rose
Over the western reaches of the Silk Road
O, the crescent moon and morning star
The crescent moon and morning star of Islam
Like the slow turning of a river
And the Silk Road went on

poems.title: The Light, Urgent Sound of Hoofbeats / Drowned by the Symphony of the Talas River
lines: The light, urgent sound of hoofbeats
Drowned by the symphony of the Talas River
The retreating army fading into history
On the riverbank a prisoner sits
White as the snow on the Tianshan
The light, urgent sound of hoofbeats

poems.title: The Three Hundred Warriors in Headlong Flight / Still Riding Eastward
lines: The three hundred warriors in headlong flight
Toward the last outposts of the Tang
Their commander fell at the river crossing
Not in retreat — but in defiance
Against the turning tide of history
On the ridge above the valley
At the world they were leaving behind
At the century they had owned

poems.title: The Snow Falls Like White Butterflies / Dancing on the Battlefield under the Waning Moon
lines: The snow falls like white butterflies
Dancing on the battlefield under the waning moon
Covering the dead with a blanket
The battlefield will be a white field
The snow falls like white butterflies
Over the dead of both sides

poems.title: He Must Have Heard the Waves of Issyk-Kul / Listening from Horseback
lines: He must have heard the waves of Issyk-Kul
As the remnants crossed the western Tianshan
The warm lake in its bowl of snow mountains
A jewel they could not stop to admire
A beauty they passed at a gallop
He must have heard the waves
And wondered if he would ever

poems.title: On This Path Where Silk Once Gleamed / A Young City Is About to Rise
lines: On this path where silk once gleamed
A young city is about to rise
Not from the ruins of war
But from the stubborn human need
To build, to trade, to connect
On this path where silk once gleamed
A young city is about to rise
On the graves of older cities
On the road that never dies

poems.title: Carrying That Lamp / Of Holy Oil from Jerusalem
lines: The Nestorian priest walked the Silk Road eastward
Christ's story traveled in his satchel
Faith packed between the trade goods

poems.title: The Blue Canal of Venice / That Little City Has Faded from View
lines: That little city has faded from view
By deserts the Venetian had never imagined
Than the markets of the Rialto

poems.title: And There Is Yarkand's / Iron Cross of St. John's Church Towering High
lines: Iron cross of St. John's Church towering high
Three prayers rising to one sky
In every tongue that traveled the road

poems.title: Tirelessly Casting / Pale, Cold Arrows into the Desert
lines: Pale, cold arrows into the desert
That still cross this ancient land

poems.title: Shooting into the Deep Forest / Shooting at the Terrified Quarry
lines: Genghis Khan hunted in these mountains
The greatest conqueror the world had known
Paused here, in the Liupan Mountains
Before his final campaign against the Tangut

poems.title: Only Then Did He Mark This Circle in Red / On His Military Map
lines: Only then did he mark this circle in red
The place where the khan would die
As though the khan still led them
The greatest deception in military history
Led by a dead man on horseback

poems.title: Only in the Distant Days of Childhood / Were Nights This Quiet
lines: Only in the distant days of childhood
On the steppe of his birth
Before the wars, before the empire
The sound of wind in the grass
The silence of the Liupan Mountains

poems.title: Qiqige Filled the Carved Pot with River Water / And Walked Away
lines: Qiqige filled the carved pot with river water
Her bare feet on the mountain stone
The water was cold and clear
But carried by girls like Qiqige

poems.title: It Was the Song His Mother Used to Sing / Sung from the Galloping Saddle
lines: It was the song his mother used to sing
A lullaby of the open steppe
Of eagle and deer and endless sky
Far from the steppe of his birth
It was the song his mother used to sing
And now it was the song

poems.title: His Steed / Still Strikes the Earth Tirelessly under the Waning Moon
lines: Still strikes the earth tirelessly under the waning moon
Even after the rider is gone
Even after the empire he built
Through the dreams of every nomad

poems.title: From the Upper Ili / Spring Wind Redolent with Apple Blossoms
lines: Spring wind redolent with apple blossoms
Where the Dzungar people once grazed their herds
Every empire that drank from its waters

poems.title: The Blood-Red Sunset / Stains the Tears Welling in Mother's Eyes
lines: Stains the tears welling in mother's eyes
She watches her sons ride west
After a century on the Volga
Losing half their people to cold and war
To the banks of the river

poems.title: Smiling / As Radiant as the Keziaerdake Flower
lines: As radiant as the Keziaerdake flower
Rides the spring pastures of the Ili
The blood has been washed from the grass
That every flower beneath her horse's hooves

poems.title: His Twenty-Two Brave Warriors Like Twenty-Two Dark Clouds / Drifting Light and Swift
lines: His twenty-two brave warriors like twenty-two dark clouds
The Ili, witness to so many battles
Carrying the reflection of dark clouds
Or might have been the sky

poems.title: Distant Mount Gegen / That Marvelous Symphony of Battle Can No Longer Be Heard
lines: That marvelous symphony of battle can no longer be heard
The stele that the emperor erected
Still stands, fading in the weather
But the mountain speaks of silence
The Ili River speaks of going on
And a monument became a stone
And a stone became part of the mountain
From the earth that holds it

poems.title: O, the Beautiful Tianshan Moon / Even Trampled, the Tianshan Moon Is Beautiful
lines: Even trampled, the Tianshan moon is beautiful
Shining on the face of Maimure
Carries across the valley like silk on wind

poems.title: Slender Fingers / Caressing a Few Amber Oleaster Fruits
lines: Caressing a few amber oleaster fruits
She sings of love and loss
Of a homeland caught between empires
Her voice the only flag she needs

poems.title: The Nightingale Has Flown / Singing an Immortal Love Song
Sapphire Eyes / Flashing Suddenly
lines: In the lamplight of the imperial chamber
She stands in the Forbidden City
But yearning for the desert air

poems.title: Weaving the Prelude / To the Future Fragrant Consort's Grand Eastward March
lines: To the future Fragrant Consort's grand eastward march
In the oleaster groves of Kashgar
Will fill the halls of empire

poems.title: O, 1771 / This Pitch-Dark Winter Night on the Volga Steppe
lines: This pitch-dark winter night on the Volga steppe
The Torghut Mongols gather in secret
Their great migration about to begin
One hundred and seventy thousand souls
Turning east in the Russian winter
Toward a homeland most have never seen
But all have heard of in song

poems.title: Is This the Ural River? / No Rolling Waves upon Its Surface
lines: No rolling waves upon its surface
The old gave up and sat down in the snow

poems.title: Qiqige Has No More Tears / She Turns on Horseback to Gaze into the Distance
lines: She turns on horseback to gaze into the distance
Half their people died on the road
Like wolves after a weakened herd

poems.title: The Kyrgyz Steppe Still Sleeps / And the Frozen Little River
lines: Barely a trickle under the ice
Through winds that strip the skin
But the Torghut do not sleep

poems.title: The Wooden Wheels of the Cart / Turning Slowly Eastward
lines: The wooden wheels of the cart
Like the prayer wheels of their temples
Through snow and mud and memory

poems.title: O, the Waves of Lake Balkhash / Have Long Surged in a Distant, Ethereal Dream
lines: O, the waves of Lake Balkhash —
Have long surged in a distant, ethereal dream
And now they surge before their eyes
Blue and real and weeping with light
They have crossed half of Eurasia
From the Volga to Lake Balkhash
From one hundred and seventy thousand
And a place to call home
That guided them through the darkest nights
The greatest migration in modern history

chapters.title: چېگرا ساداقەتلىك ئوغلى ئاھ-زار بىلەن يىغلايدۇ

poems.title: This Moon / Floating on the Dark-Blue Surface of Lake Balkhash
lines: Floating on the dark-blue surface of Lake Balkhash
Is the same moon that shone on Lin Zexu
When the emperor sent him into exile
The man who burned the opium
Now walks the irrigation canals of Xinjiang
Doing the work of a common laborer
And a loyal subject's unbroken spirit

poems.title: Rubbing a Page / Of the Ancient Western Regions' Historical Scroll
lines: Of the ancient Western Regions' historical scroll
Lin Zexu traced the old canals
That Han soldiers had dug two thousand years before
Learning the language of the desert

poems.title: No Wonder the Stern Summit / Will Not Show Its Bright Face
lines: Will not show its bright face
As if mourning the state of the empire
Lin Zexu looked up at the mountains
Of burning ships and burning opium
Of an empire that punished him for being right
Of what the court has become

poems.title: Beneath the Frontier Moon / A Gleaming Arrow Flies Swift
lines: Lin Zexu's will was an arrow
He would never see Canton again
He would die on the road back from exile
An old man in a borrowed carriage
The gleaming arrow of his legacy

poems.title: Tenderly Stroking / The Ash-Brown Eagle Feather a Kazakh Herdsman Gave Him
lines: The ash-brown eagle feather a Kazakh herdsman gave him
The exile walks the final road
Of the Silk Road's long poem
All leading here, to this moment
To a man stroking an eagle feather

poems.title: Singing Mournfully at the Frontier / The Ancient Post Road Stretching beneath His Feet
lines: The ancient post road stretching beneath his feet
In the longest poem ever written

poems.title: Clouds Obscure the Tianshan Moon / You Softly Wipe Away Your Tears
lines: You softly wipe away your tears
For this land that has seen
So much courage and so much sorrow
So much silk and so much blood

poems.title: Lai Lai Lai / The Loving Sayram Lake Brims with Fragrant Wine
lines: The loving Sayram Lake brims with fragrant wine
Raise your cup to the mountains
To every traveler who has passed
Through this land of snow and fire

poems.title: He Once Loved a Blue Sea in the South / And Loves Too These Thousand Crystal Peaks of the West
lines: He once loved a blue sea in the south
And loves too these thousand crystal peaks of the west
Who in the Western Regions does not know thee?
Of blood-sweating horses and wild poppies
Of monks and generals and princesses
Of envoys who would not kneel
Who in the Western Regions does not know thee?
Every grain of desert sand knows
